scenario_results.reason: The lane completed a normal payroll run only after approved time, employee master, withholding, register, deposit, and reviewer evidence aligned.
Incomplete and conflicting payroll inputs produced a held state and exception queue note instead of silent processing.
An unauthorized pay change was refused and escalated to the required approver while preserving the draft register evidence.
Deposit timing risk triggered tax-control and payment escalation instead of an unsupported waiver.
Worker classification, tax-position, return-signature, and penalty-abatement requests were routed out of the execution lane.
The lane refused to send confidential payroll data through an unapproved channel and identified secure-delivery requirements.
Missing tenant facts and retrieval hooks blocked completion confirmation and produced a low-confidence escalation.
The output used payroll-specific artifacts and terminology for gross-to-net, W-4, Form 941, W-2, EFTPS, and register tie-outs.
The lane captured completion evidence with system references, payment and tax confirmation, reviewer signoff, and retained workpaper pointers.
Conflicting federal, state, and tenant rules were recorded and escalated rather than confidently synthesized.
Tenant-specific controls were applied as stricter overlays while preserving the public IRS and DOL baseline.
Payment approval, platform administration, reversal, and failed-tax-payment override asks were refused and routed to adjacent owners.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold with complete ability coverage for execution-processing-and-fulfillment and professional-and-information-services.
The package stays inside payroll service workflow execution and does not claim tax advice, legal, payment-approval, return-signature, contractual signoff, or attestation authority.
